title: Vex Asteroid Belt

The '''Vex Asteroid Belt''', also known as '''the Belt''', was a part of the [[Vex system/Legends|Vex system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]]. Featuring [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] bases and a population of [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Spacer/Legends|jocks]], explorers, and [[Prospector/Legends|prospectors]], the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] was originally [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] with the goal of exploring the nearby [[Monsua Nebula/Legends|Monsua Nebula]]. Various deals of questionable legality could be made with relative ease with the inhabitants of the Vex Asteroid Belt, who were collectively known as the [[Belters (Vex Asteroid Belt)|Belters]].

==Description==
The Vex Asteroid Belt, also referred to simply as the Belt,<ref name="Endor" /> was located in the [[Vex system/Legends|Vex system]] and lay within the [[Inner Moddell systems]] area of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]],<ref name="EAOC Vex" /> one of the [[Sector/Legends|subsectors]] making up the [[Inner Zuma Region]]<ref name="TEA" /> within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Zuma sector/Legends|Zuma sector]].<ref name="EAOC Vex" /> Along with the rest of the Vex [[Star system/Legends|system]], the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] was linked by [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] to the [[Vasha system/Legends|Vasha system]] as well as the [[Firrerre system/Legends|Firrerre system]]<ref name="TEA" /> in the [[Sugai sector/Legends|Sugai sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The Vex Asteroid Belt consisted of<ref name="Endor" /> tumbling,<ref name="Tessent">{{GamerCite|9|Race for the Tessent}}</ref> barren [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] with various [[Gravity/Legends|gravitic]] pull.<ref name="Endor" />

==History==
The Vex Asteroid Belt was originally [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] with the purpose of [[Scout/Legends|exploring]] the nearby [[Monsua Nebula/Legends|Monsua Nebula]]. During the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the Belt's [[kiriswing joint]]s and [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] were patroned by the decorated yet eccentric [[Nova Scout]] [[Ecclesis von der Agar]]. He often recruited new arrivals to the Belt as apprentice scouts but frequently brought trouble for them.<ref name="Endor" /> It was also rumored that one of the two [[Alsakan Tessent|valuable]] [[Ayrou Tessent|sculptures]] known as the [[Tessent]]s could be found floating among the Belt's asteroids.<ref name="Tessent" />

==Inhabitants==
The Vex system's asteroid belt was home to a population of three thousand [[Human/Legends|Humans]], who were collectively known as [[Belters (Vex Asteroid Belt)|Belters]]. They had no over-arching government, imported [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]] and technology from elsewhere and primarily consisted of quarreling [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Spacer/Legends|jocks]], scouts, and [[Prospector/Legends|prospectors]]. The scouts in the Vex Asteroid Belt mostly operated independently, although the [[New Republic Survey Corps/Legends|New Republic Survey Corps]] also maintained a small outpost on the belt's largest asteroid.<ref name="Endor" />

A sufficient amount of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] allowed visitors to the Belt to sell illegal cargo, hide a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] or a fugitive, or strike other shady deals. The Belters asked few questions about the legality of such agreements, and were not interested at all in the dealings of the inhabitants of the nearby [[Fisher/Legends|fisherman's]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vex Major]], whom they derogatorily referred to as "mudballers."<ref name="Endor" />

==Locations==
The Vex Asteroid Belt hosted [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] scout bases and a total of twelve [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]]: two [[Standard Class starport|standard-class]] and ten of limited services.<ref name="Endor" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Vex Asteroid Belt was introduced in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Endor" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> Within the magazine, the asteroid belt was mentioned in the article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]" as well as the [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' adventure "[[Race for the Tessent]]."<ref name="Endor" /><ref name="Tessent" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Vex Asteroid Belt in grid square H-16.<ref name="TEA" />
